business.headline: EPFO 3.0: 5 PF mistakes that could delay your transfer when switching jobsChanging jobs comes with plenty of paperwork, and transferring your PF is one task you shouldn't ignore. While EPFO 3.0 promises a faster and smoother process, a few simple mistakes could still delay your money. Are you making any of them?

Gold price has crashed nearly Rs 50,000 from its peak. Will prices fall further?Gold has slipped sharply from its MCX record high as investors reassess the US rate outlook. The fall has given buyers some relief, but volatility remains tied to inflation, oil and Federal Reserve signals.

BAT to slash 9,000 jobs amid shift to smoke-free productsThe move is part of the company's wider plan to reduce costs while investing more in smoke-free products such as vaping devices and nicotine pouches.

Nearly Rs 9,000 crore in unclaimed insurance: How to check if any is yoursNearly Rs 8,974 crore in insurance money remains unclaimed with insurers across India. Old policies, missing nominee details and outdated records could leave rightful dues untouched for years.

Samsung, SK Hynix's $518 billion AI bet: Why the chip giants are expandingAs the AI boom fuels a global race for computing power, Samsung and SK Hynix are making one of their biggest expansion bets yet. Here's why the chip giants are investing $518 billion.

Home sales slip 6%: Why are buyers pulling back across top cities?ANAROCK said housing sales across the top seven cities fell in Q2 2026 even as new launches rose. The data showed a more balanced market as war-linked uncertainty and IT sector concerns weighed on buyers.

One missed EMI, endless harassment? The dark side of digital lending boomDigital lending has made borrowing faster and easier than ever before. Yet behind the promise of instant cash lies a troubling reality: for some borrowers, a single missed EMI can lead to harassment, intimidation and financial distress.

Think your Rs 5 lakh health insurance cover is enough? It may not beA new analysis of more than one lakh policyholders by insurance platform CoverSure has found that a majority of people above the age of 50 have health insurance coverage of less than Rs 5 lakh. Even more worrying, 17% of policyholders with chronic medical conditions also have a sum insured below Rs 5 lakh.

ITR filing 2026: July's biggest income tax deadlines explainedJuly brings six key income tax deadlines, including the 31 July ITR filing cut-off. Missing them can lead to late fees, interest and limits on carrying forward losses.

Sensex, Nifty open flat as crude stays low, FII mood improvesThe BSE Sensex was up 48.58 points, or 0.06%, at 77,149.05 at around 9:20 am, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 44.65 points, or 0.19%, to trade at 24,100.65 as of 9:23 am.

Sensex, Nifty today: Will markets rise or fall after a 3-day break?GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 24,093 around 8:07 am, indicating that the Nifty 50 could open close to Friday's closing level of 24,056, suggesting a muted start to the trading session.

Gold prices fall: Indians sell old jewellery as fears of deeper correction growAs gold prices retreat from record highs, Indian households are selling old jewellery to lock in gains. The trend is lifting organised recycling volumes and may help reduce reliance on imports.

India reclaims 5th spot among world's biggest stock markets after Asian selloffIndia has regained the world's fifth-largest stock market position after Taiwan and South Korea slipped. The shift highlights how easing crude prices, foreign buying and regional corrections are reshaping rankings.

Kotak Mahindra Bank MD and CEO Ashok Vaswani to step down at year-endKotak Mahindra Bank has begun searching for a successor after CEO Ashok Vaswani said he will not seek reappointment when his term ends in December 2026. The move comes as the lender seeks faster growth after regulatory scrutiny and may keep investors focused on leadership continuity.

NSE, Jio IPOs have retail investors excited. But are big listings worth the hype?NSE and Jio are already generating plenty of excitement. But before investors join the rush, recent IPO history offers an important lesson about hype, valuations and long-term returns. Read on to find out why big listings don't always translate into big gains.

Will life insurance pay after a fire? Here's what every family should knowRecent fire tragedies have triggered questions over whether life insurance pays after accidental deaths. Experts say standard term plans generally cover such deaths unless false disclosures or policy exclusions apply.

Asian markets crash, will Dalal Street feel the aftershock on Monday?From South Korea's Kospi to Japan's Nikkei, Asian stock markets have tumbled sharply, leaving investors on edge. As Dalal Street prepares to reopen after a three-day holiday on Monday, will the BSE and NSE also open deep in the red?

From ITR filing to TDS: 3 major tax deadlines in July 2026 you shouldn't missWhether you're a salaried employee, a business owner or an employer, keeping track of these dates can help you avoid last-minute stress. Here are three key tax deadlines you should not miss in July 2026.

Will your family get your EPF pension after your death? Here's what you need to knowNo one likes to think about what happens after they're gone. But if you're an EPF member, knowing whether your family can continue receiving your pension could make a big difference to their financial future.

Will DA rise before the 8th Pay Commission? Here's what the latest data suggestsThe wait for the 8th Pay Commission continues, but all eyes are now on the next DA revision. As inflation remains elevated and the July review draws closer, is another DA hike just around the corner?

entertainment.headline: K Bhagyaraj's son Shanthnu, daughter Sharanya pay emotional tribute to late father Shanthnu Bhagyaraj and Sharanya Bhagyaraj shared emotional memories of their father, K Bhagyaraj, days after his death. Their posts drew condolences and underscored the filmmaker's enduring bond with family and Tamil cinema.

Suriya reunites with TJ Gnanavel as Hombale Films announces new project Hombale Films announced its maiden collaboration with Suriya. Directed by TJ Gnanavel, the yet-untitled film stars Kayadu Lohar and features music by Sai Abhyankkar.

Tom Holland hints at Miles Morales' Marvel debut ahead of Brand New Day release Actor Tom Holland said he wants Miles Morales to enter the Marvel Cinematic Universe. The remarks have renewed focus on the character's live-action future before Spider-Man: Brand New Day.

Suriya not interested in politics: Actor's fan club clarifies ex-member's remark The All India Suriya Fans Club said actor Suriya has no plans to enter politics after remarks by former member R Veeramani triggered speculation. The organisation said his comments were personal and urged people to ignore rumours.

Anushka Ranjan, Aditya Seal celebrate godh bharai ahead of welcoming first child Anushka Ranjan shared photos from her Godh Bharai ceremony with Aditya Seal and their families. The celebration came days after the couple announced they are expecting their first child.

House of the Dragon S3 Ep 2 review: Rhaenyra's glory comes with her greatest grief Spoilers - you have been warned. Rhaenyra claims the Iron Throne after reaching King's Landing, even as Jace's death engulfs her in grief. The episode frames her ascent as a hollow victory, with the war still raging and the realm still divided.

Shraddha Kapoor's Welcome to The Jungle date night with partner Rahul Mody. Video A viral video showed Shraddha Kapoor and boyfriend Rahul Mody enjoying Welcome To The Jungle in a theatre. The clip has revived interest in their relationship after several public appearances together.

Actor Akanksha Chamola says no to love after separation from Gaurav Khanna Actor Akanksha Chamola has revealed that she and Gaurav Khanna have been separated for over a year and are headed for divorce. She shared that after a decade-long relationship, she is not ready to fall in love again and wants to explore life on her own terms.

Kalki 2898 AD director Nag Ashwin, wife Priyanka Dutt are expecting second child Nag Ashwin and Priyanka Dutt announced they are expecting their second child through a family photo on social media. The director's post drew congratulatory posts from friends and fans.

Cocktail 2 box office Day 10: Shahid's 4th Rs 100 crore grosser worldwide Director Homi Adajania's Cocktail 2 earned over Rs 99 crore at the Indian box office on Monday. The romantic drama has joined the league of Shahid Kapoor's films that have crossed Rs 100 crore mark.

Kannada actor Sharmiela Mandre engaged to Tamil producer Sudhan Sundaram Kannada actor-producer Sharmiela Mandre got engaged to Tamil producer and Passion Studios founder Sudhan Sundaram in an intimate ceremony in Bengaluru attended by family and close friends. Photos from the ceremony are now doing the rounds.

Say no: Amaal Mallik reacts to Ketan Agarwal's death, has a message for women Singer Amaal Mallik reacted to Ketan Agarwal's death with a sharp post questioning why the marriage was not stopped earlier. His remarks came as police examined allegations of a planned murder conspiracy and a grieving family sought answers.

Awarapan 2 teaser: Emraan Hashmi's Shivam is back; Disha Patani joins sequel Actor Emraan Hashmi returns as Shivam Pandit in the Awarapan 2 teaser, with actors Disha Patani and Shabana Azmi featuring prominently. The footage revives the original's brooding mood while setting up a larger world of vengeance, romance and heartbreak.

Rajamouli elated as 93-year Oscar winner Costa-Gavras watches his films for 8 hours SS Rajamouli was honoured at the Cinematheque Francaise in Paris during a screening session of his films. The moment drew added attention after 93-year-old Oscar-award-winning filmmaker Costa-Gavras stayed for eight hours to watch the films and masterclass.

Bengali TV show's cockroach eating scene sparks deja vu of Karanvir Bohra's viral moment A Bengali TV show, Kamala Nibas, has sparked outrage online after a scene showed a groom dipping a cockroach in milk and eating it. Viewers linked the moment to Karanvir Bohra's old Viraj Dobriyal scene and questioned its shock value.

Actor Radikaa Sarathkumar slams paparazzi: Funeral a farewell, not photo op Actor Radikaa Sarathkumar confronted people filming K Bhagyaraj's grieving family during his funeral in Chennai. Her intervention underscored a wider call for privacy and restraint during a moment of personal loss.

Explained: Farrhana Bhatt's family row, alleged sister's claims, mother's response The family dispute involving Bigg Boss 19 runner-up Farrhana Bhatt has surfaced with conflicting claims from a woman alleging to be her sister and Farrhana's mother refuting those allegations. The controversy has sparked social media discussions, but Farrhana has yet to comment on the matter.

Saif Ali Khan on raising sons with Kareena beyond religion: One God, many names Actor Saif Ali Khan opened up about the conversations he has with his sons, Taimur and Jehangir, about religion. He reflected on his interfaith upbringing and the values he hopes to pass on to his family.

In the name of Rama: Fresh Ramayana spins face box office test Ramayana, Varanasi and Alpha aim to recast elements of the epic. But recent experiments with the epic have strived to find takers among a deeply reverential audience.

general news.headline: Not our lawyer, threatening us: Siya Goyal's brother adds twist to murder probeSpeaking exclusively to India Today, Sahil said neither he nor his family had authorised lawyer Aashutosh Srivastava to represent them in the case. He further alleged that Srivastava may have obtained Siya's signature "by deceit", though he did not elaborate on the circumstances.

Gold prices fall: Indians sell old jewellery as fears of deeper correction growAs gold prices retreat from record highs, Indian households are selling old jewellery to lock in gains. The trend is lifting organised recycling volumes and may help reduce reliance on imports.

Curious case of Rs 58 lakh recovered by Ram Temple Trust before theft probe beganSources claim temple authorities recovered Rs 58 lakh before the FIR in the Ram Temple donation theft case, while the remaining money was returned through bank transfers. The case has since led to arrests, Trust resignations, and an SIT probe into alleged lapses in donation handling and oversight.

Pakistan suspends Geo News for 15 days over offensive Muharram broadcastPakistan has suspended Karachi-based Geo News for 15 days over a Muharram programme that authorities said showed objectionable religious visuals. The channel apologised and removed the content, but the case has revived concerns over Shia-Sunni sectarian sensitivities and media controls under Pakistan's civilian-military hybrid regime.

Vedant Srivastava vs CBSE over Physics marks: Lies or misrepresentation?The dispute over Vedant Srivastava's revised Physics marks has now shifted beyond the answer-sheet mix-up itself, with both sides offering differing accounts of whether the nine additional marks were awarded and what they represent.

Union Minister Pabitra Margherita, Bihar Governor to attend Khamenei's funeralIndia will be represented by Minister of State for External Affairs Pabitra Margherita and Bihar Governor Lt Gen (Retd) Syed Ata Hasnain at the funeral of former Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, with the rites scheduled from July 4 to 9 in Iran.

3 murders in one night rock Bengaluru: 2 women killed, history-sheeter hackedBengaluru saw three fatal attacks on Sunday night, including two women murdered in separate incidents and a history-sheeter hacked to death on a busy road. Police are probing suspected relationship-linked killings in the women's cases and gang rivalry in the third incident.

ISKCON removes top Kolkata priest, bars him from speaking on its behalfRadharaman Das, who had become one of ISKCON's most outspoken public figures on contentious issues, says the action stemmed from his public interventions. ISKCON, however, says the step was taken because he repeatedly violated organisational procedures, acted without authorisation and took positions contrary to the organisation's official mandate.

In the name of Rama: Fresh Ramayana spins face box office testRamayana, Varanasi and Alpha aim to recast elements of the epic. But recent experiments with the epic have strived to find takers among a deeply reverential audience.

CBSE clarifies 3-language rule: One-time relief for Classes 7-9, no R3 board examCBSE has issued guidelines to implement NEP 2020's three-language policy from the 2026-27 session. The board has built in relaxations and school support to ease the transition and limit exam pressure.

What are Indian Army's Integrated Battle Groups that will change the way it fights?The Indian Army is set to begin a phased rollout of its Integrated Battle Groups (IBGs) in a month, reportedly starting with the XVII Corps. The self-contained combat formations are expected to transform how the Army deploys, mobilises and fights, with an emphasis on speed, flexibility and integrated operations.

Iran or the US: Who will pull in the bigger crowds this week?While America celebrates 250 years of independence, Iran prepares a massive farewell for Ayatollah Khamenei as the two countries negotiate a fragile peace after months of war.

Why Delhi feels like a furnace just before the monsoonDelhi posted its hottest morning in nearly two years as monsoon moisture built over the city. The rising humidity pushed the feels-like temperature far above the actual reading ahead of the rains.

India women self-destruct, have no one else to blame for T20 World Cup exitWomen's T20 World Cup, INDW vs AUSW: Harmanpreet Kaur's India crashed out of the tournament after a six-wicket defeat to Australia at Lord's on Sunday. The loss highlighted familiar shortcomings in bowling, fielding and decision-making, exposing the areas India must address if they are to consistently challenge the world's best.
Why AC explosions are becoming more common in Delhi-NCRA Noida AC blast has raised safety concerns as the region faces extreme heat. Here's why air conditioners explode, and how to prevent them.

Death penalty for 65-year-old for rape, murder of girl, 3; verdict in 60 daysA 65-year-old man was given a death penalty by a Pune court for luring a 3-year-old girl to a cattle shed in Nasrapur village and then raping and murdering her on May 1, 2026.

One last wicket, one final swing: Ben Stokes gives Trent Bridge a goodbye to rememberENG vs NZ: Ben Stokes' final innings for England lasted just 20 balls, but it was filled with everything that defined his career. After announcing his shock retirement, the England captain signed off with a fearless 30, a standing ovation and a guard of honour from New Zealand.

Delayed or denied? Sooryavanshi snub splits opinion after Ireland shockerVaibhav Sooryavanshi was benched throughout India's series defeat to Ireland, splitting opinion among former players. Gavaskar, Kaif and Tiwary back his inclusion. Ashwin backs the management. The debate now shadows India's England tour from July 1.

Ford rehiring old engineers after AI failed at workFord thought artificial intelligence could solve its quality problems, but the technology fell short without decades of engineering know-how. The automaker has now brought back hundreds of veteran engineers to train both younger employees and its AI, and the strategy is already paying off.

Will Mojtaba Khamenei be seen in public for the first time at his father's funeral?Iran is preparing for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's state funeral. The current attention now is whether Ayatollah's son, the present Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, makes his first public appearance. Mojtaba's continued absence has been increasing questions and speculation over his health, authority, and the leadership transition.

Has Delhi's Sarojini Nagar lost its charm or did it just get better?The short answer is no - Sarojini Nagar has not lost its charm despite what several Instagram videos suggest. Read on to know why we say so after a Sunday visit to the market.

Welcome To The Jungle box office Day 3: Akshay's film nears Rs 100 crore globallyActor Akshay Kumar's Welcome To The Jungle continued its strong box office run over the weekend. The comedy film has now moved close to the Rs 100-crore mark worldwide.

One missed EMI, endless harassment? The dark side of digital lending boomDigital lending has made borrowing faster and easier than ever before. Yet behind the promise of instant cash lies a troubling reality: for some borrowers, a single missed EMI can lead to harassment, intimidation and financial distress.

India makes hydrogen from nuclear heat, not electricity. Why scientists are thrilledIndia has inaugurated the world's first plant that makes hydrogen using nuclear reactor heat instead of electricity. Built at Kalpakkam, the copper-chlorine cycle points to a cleaner, round-the-clock route to green hydrogen.

sports.headline: I am there: Srikanth confident of brighter days after US Open runner-up finish Kidambi Srikanth finished runner-up at the US Open after a hard-fought three-game final against Su Li-yang. The run strengthened his belief that his game is close to breaking through again.

Brazil vs Japan: FIFA World Cup prepares for potential classic Brazil return to action in the FIFA World Cup, as the 5-time champions take on Asia's best football team Japan. Will Brazil's Samba outmatch the underdogs, or will the Samurai cut down the Brazilian flair?

Was Ben Stokes forced to retire? England captain breaks silence on selfish call England captain Ben Stokes dropped a mid-match retirement bombshell at Trent Bridge, telling teammates the reasons could wait, before opening up on burnout and the nightclub controversy that clouded his final weeks.

Wimbledon: Emma Raducanu withdraws, fit-again Sinner ready to battle heat Emma Raducanu has withdrawn from Wimbledon after scans confirmed a stress fracture in her lower right leg. Jannik Sinner, meanwhile, says rising temperatures are reshaping preparations for his title defence.

Sooryavanshi should wait: India remain stubborn ahead of England T20I series Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is ready for international cricket but needs to bide his time, said India assistant coach Ryan ten Doeschate after the team's historic series loss against Ireland. Does this mean that Vaibhav will not play in England as well?

Wimbledon Day 1: Sinner, Djokovic start as London heat eases Wimbledon 2026 opens with Jannik Sinner, Novak Djokovic and Aryna Sabalenka leading Day 1 on Centre Court. Their starts come as London's heat eases, though changed conditions remain a factor.

Days after Jaspal Rana's death, shooter's mother dies of cardiac arrest Less than three weeks after Indian shooting legend Jaspal Rana's death, his family suffered another tragedy as his mother Shyama Devi passed away following prolonged health complications and hospitalisation.

Cape Verde captain under investigation for alleged rape ahead of Argentina clash Cape Verde's historic FIFA World Cup run has been overshadowed by serious allegations. Brazilian publication Globo is reporting that the team's captain, Ryan Mendes, is under investigation in New Zealand over an alleged sexual assault during the FIFA Series in March.

India women self-destruct, have no one else to blame for T20 World Cup exit Women's T20 World Cup, INDW vs AUSW: Harmanpreet Kaur's India crashed out of the tournament after a six-wicket defeat to Australia at Lord's on Sunday. The loss highlighted familiar shortcomings in bowling, fielding and decision-making, exposing the areas India must address if they are to consistently challenge the world's best.

Delayed or denied? Sooryavanshi snub splits opinion after Ireland shocker Vaibhav Sooryavanshi was benched throughout India's series defeat to Ireland, splitting opinion among former players. Gavaskar, Kaif and Tiwary back his inclusion. Ashwin backs the management. The debate now shadows India's England tour from July 1.

Canada end brave Bafana Bafana dream with stoppage-time heartbreak Stephen Eustaquio scored deep in stoppage time as Canada beat South Africa 1-0 in Los Angeles. The defeat ended Bafana Bafana's brave World Cup run, while Canada reached the Round of 16 for the first time.

Serena Williams reveals why she accepted Wimbledon singles comebackSerena Williams admits she nearly walked away from one final Wimbledon singles opportunity before convincing herself to embrace the moment. The 23-time Grand Slam champion will return to Centre Court after almost four years away, insisting she could not let the chance slip by.

Lack joy and happiness: Quaresma explains Ronaldo-led Portugal's strugglesFIFA World Cup 2026: Portugal scraped into the knockout stage after a flat goalless draw with Colombia. The stale display has intensified scrutiny of Cristiano Ronaldo, midfield control and the side's wider cohesion issues.

South Korea coach resigns after World Cup exit as President Lee lashes outFIFA World Cup 2026: Hong Myung-bo resigned after South Korea crashed out in the World Cup group stage. The exit provoked President Lee's attack on football administration and renewed scrutiny of the appointment process.

Harmanpreet to continue as India captain? India coach Amol Muzumdar opens upWomen's T20 World Cup, INDW vs AUSW: India head coach Amol Muzumdar addressed questions over Harmanpreet Kaur's future as captain following India's early exit from the Women's T20 World Cup.

One last wicket, one final swing: Ben Stokes gives Trent Bridge a goodbye to rememberENG vs NZ: Ben Stokes' final innings for England lasted just 20 balls, but it was filled with everything that defined his career. After announcing his shock retirement, the England captain signed off with a fearless 30, a standing ovation and a guard of honour from New Zealand.

RSA 0-1 CAN Highlights: Canada advance to Round of 16 with late winSouth Africa (RSA) vs Canada (CAN), FIFA World Cup 2026 Highlights: Canada left it painfully late, but Stephen Eustaquio's stoppage-time strike finally broke South African resistance to send Jesse Marsch's side into the Round of 16 with a 1-0 win over South Africa in LA.

India not good enough against top teams: Harmanpreet Kaur after early exitWomen's T20 World Cup, INDW vs AUSW: Harmanpreet Kaur admitted India need to raise their standards against the top teams and rethink their approach after their semifinal hopes ended with a defeat to Australia.

T20 world champions India lose series 0-2 in Ireland as 16-series streak endsIreland defended 154 and beat India by one run in Belfast to complete a 2-0 T20I series sweep. The result ended India's 16-series bilateral winning streak and exposed a brittle top order.

ODI champs India suffer Women's T20 World Cup heartbreak, crash out before semisWomen's T20 World Cup 2026, INDW vs AUSW: Harmanpreet Kaur's India failed to advance to the semis after losing to Australia by six wickets in their first-ever T20I at the iconic Lord's Cricket Ground. From Group A, South Africa and Australia went through to the semis.

Rohit Yadav hurls world's second-best javelin throw of 2026, seals Asian Games berthRohit Yadav produced a career-best 87.05m throw to win gold at the Interstate Athletics Championships, qualify for the 2026 Asian Games and register the second-best javelin throw in the world this season.

Virat Kohli cheers for Harmanpreet's record Lord's fifty, attends game with AnushkaVirat Kohli, Shikhar Dhawan, Anushka Sharma, Sophie Shine and Ravi Shastri were among the notable personalities at Lord's as India faced Australia in a crucial Women's T20 World Cup clash on Sunday.

Expected him to play both games: Sunil Gavaskar surprised at Sooryavanshi's Ireland omissionIND vs IRE: Sunil Gavaskar said he was surprised that Vaibhav Sooryavanshi was not given a debut in the Ireland T20I series despite his strong IPL performances. However, he believes the teenager remains in India's plans and is likely to get his opportunity during the upcoming England white-ball series.

Formula 1: George Russell wins in Austria, Verstappen and Antonelli join him on podiumFormula 1: George Russell won the Austrian Grand Prix from pole despite suffering a drinks system failure and extreme heat conditions, holding off Max Verstappen and Kimi Antonelli. The result trimmed Antonelli's championship lead and extended Mercedes' strong run in both the drivers' and constructors' standings.

Ben Stokes announces shock retirement from international midway during a TestEngland captain Ben Stokes has announced his retirement from international cricket and will draw the curtains down on his career after the ongoing Test against New Zealand at Trent Bridge. A World Cup winner, Ashes hero and architect of England's Bazball revolution, Stokes leaves behind one of the greatest legacies in modern English cricket.

South Africa beat Bangladesh: What India now need to reach Women's T20 World Cup semisWomen's T20 World Cup, IND vs AUS: After South Africa's four-wicket win over Bangladesh, what do India need to do against Australia at Lord's to secure a place in the semifinals?

Hopefully, I'll meet him: Novak Djokovic eyes Virat Kohli meet on India visitNovak Djokovic says he plans to visit India and finally meet Virat Kohli. The long-distance sporting friendship now edges towards a public moment as Wimbledon opens and Djokovic chases history.

No Sooryavanshi debut in Ireland, Suryansh and Prince get India T20I capsIRE vs IND: India brought in Suryansh Shedge and Prince Yadav for the second T20I in Belfast. The changes followed Ireland's landmark win, while Vaibhav Sooryavanshi remained on the bench.

IND vs AUS Highlights: India crash out after 7-wicket loss to AustraliaIndia (IND-W) vs Australia (AUS-W) Highlights, Women's T20 World Cup: India crashed out of the tournament after losing to Australia by six wickets on Sunday at the Lord's Cricket Ground. Chasing 171, the Aussies romped home with five balls to spare.

2nd T20I Highlights: India's 16 series unbeaten streak ends, Ireland win 2-0India (IND) vs Ireland (IRE), 2nd T20I Highlights: Ireland pulled off a famous one-run win to stun the T20 world champions in Belfast. The victory ended India's 16-series unbeaten run and handed them their first bilateral T20I series defeat since 2023.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
